summary: Comprehensive testing of Nameh.me email service V2 completed. All 21 backend API tests passed (100%). All frontend features working correctly including label filtering fix, folder navigation, email viewing, compose, settings, and auth flows. No performance issues or artificial delays found.
passed_tests: Backend: 21/21 API tests passed (100%)
Auth: Demo login (demo@nameh.me/demo123) works correctly
Auth: Registration flow creates new user and logs in
Auth: Invalid credentials rejected with 401
Auth: Duplicate email registration rejected with 409
Auth page: Split layout with logo on left, form on right
Logo: Clean SVG envelope without background box
Mail dashboard: 3-column layout (sidebar, email list, reading pane)
Sidebar: Folders display with unread counts (Inbox 2, Spam 2)
Sidebar: Labels display with colored dots (Work, Personal, Project, Finance, Travel)
Label filtering: Work label shows 2 emails
Label filtering: Personal label shows 1 email
Label filtering: Project label shows 3 emails
Label filtering: Click again to deselect returns all 10 inbox emails
Folder navigation: Sent (4), Drafts (1), Spam (2), Trash (1), Starred (2)
Category tabs: Primary (5), Social (2), Promotions (1), Updates (2)
Email view: Subject, sender avatar, date, body, label badges displayed
Star toggle: Works instantly on email rows
Checkbox selection: Works on individual emails
Select All: Selects/deselects all emails
Bulk actions: Archive, trash, mark read buttons appear
Search: Filters emails correctly (tested 'security' query)
Compose modal: Opens, fills To/Subject/Body, minimize/restore works
Settings panel: Language, Font, Signature options displayed
Logout: Returns to auth page
No artificial setTimeout delays in email loading
Performance: Instant folder switching with cache
seed_data_creation: Backend seeds 17 emails (10 inbox, 3 sent, 1 draft, 2 spam, 1 trash) with labels assigned
context_for_next_testing_agent: All features working. Label filtering uses client-side filtering via activeLabel state in Mail.jsx. Backend uses in-memory dictionaries (MOCKED). Test user registration creates TEST_ prefixed users.
mocked_api_note: Backend uses in-memory dictionaries (users_db, emails_db, labels_db, settings_db) instead of real database. All data is seeded on server start.
